creator: gslin@gslin.org
text:
On Mon, Aug 11, 2008 at 09:38:58AM +0200, Lupe Christoph wrote:
> On Monday, 2008-08-11 at 04:17:22 +0800, Gea-Suan Lin wrote:
> > On Sun, Aug 10, 2008 at 09:13:44PM +0200, Lupe Christoph wrote:
> > > I still debate the necessity to use a scarce resource like a UID and a
> > > GID for this port.
> > Fixed uid/gid is an important issue in NIS or LDAP environment, since
> > this port will install "munin" user to max UID available (by
> > /usr/sbin/pw), it's possible to make conflict uid.
> But both ports will accept a pre-existing UID and GID. So you just have
> to make your own "munin" user and group and it will happily accept
> those.

Thanks! I didn't get this idea before.  I'll try to use this method to
deploy it.

-- 
* Gea-Suan Lin                                    http://blog.gslin.org/
* If you cannot convince them, confuse them.           -- Harry S Truman
